original: Dmitri Mendeleevpresents the first periodic table of the elements to the Russian Chemical Society
Özlem Türeci, German scientist (BioNTech - created 1st mRNA-based vaccine for COVID-19), born in Siegen, West Germany
Alexey Molchanov, Russian freediver (25 world records), born in Volgograd, Russia
Susan Birmingham makes then world's loudest recorded human shout (120 dB)
Silly Putty goes on sale in the US
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champ in Hartford won by Denise Biellmann
NBA highest scoring game: San Antonio beat Milwaukee 171-166 (3 OT)
World's oldest message in a bottle is found in Western Australia, thrown from the German ship Paula 132 years ago on June 12, 1886
Robin (Dick Grayson) debuts as Batman's sidekick in issue 38 of Detective Comics by DC Comics
NASA's Dawn space probe enters orbit around the dwarf planet Ceres

In his 10th title defense, WBA light welterweight champion Antonio Cervantes of Colombia is beaten by 17-year-old Puerto Rican boxerWilfred Benítezin a 15-round split decision in San Juan, Puerto Rico; Benitez youngest ever world champion
Sônia Guajajara, Brazilian indigenous activist and politician (1st indigenous person on a presidential ticket in Brazil), born in  Araribóia Indigenous Land, Brazil[1]
Millicent Simmonds, American deaf actress (A Quiet Place), born in Bountiful, Utah
Farthest radio signal heard (Pioneer IV, 400,000 miles)
USSR's Vega 1 flies by Halley's Comet at 8,889 km
American WWII aircraft carrier USS Lexington rediscovered in Australia's Coral Sea, lost during 1942 Battle of the Coral Sea
Spacecraft Athena, carrying a NASA ice drill, built by Intuitive Machines, Texas, lands on its side on the Moon, ending its mission
Future undisputed world heavyweight boxing championMike TysonKOs Hector Mercedes at 1:47 in round 1 of 4 in Albany, NY in his first professional fight
LeBron Jamesscores his 32,293rd point in the 2nd quarter of a Lakers' 115-99 loss to Denver in LA to pass Michael Jordan into 4th place on the NBA all-time point scoring list
Pete Gray [Wyshner], American baseball outfielder (First of three one-armed men to play MLB; St Louis Browns), born in Nanticoke, Pennsylvania (d. 2002)
Ed Whitlock, Canadian athlete (first 70+ to run a marathon in less than 3 hours 2:59:10 in 2003), born in London, England (d. 2017)
Ning Zetao, Chinese swimmer (World C'ship gold 100m freestyle 2015), born in Zhengzhou, China
WWII: British and Canadian sailors boards abandoned German U-boat U-774 seeking Enigma code machine, but are forced to scuttle the sub when efforts to tow it fail
Joseph von Fraunhofer, German physicist and optical lens manufacturer (studied Sun's spectrum, invented the spectroscope), born in Straubing, Bavaria (d. 1826)
The Beatles release single "Let it Be" in UK
Babe Ruthsigns 3 year contract with NY Yankees at $52,000 a year
Test Cricket debut of Indian cricket greatSunil Gavaskarversus West Indies at Port-of-Spain; 125 Tests, 10,122 runs @ 51.12; 34 Test centuries
Harry Coover, American chemist and inventor of Superglue, born in Newark, Delaware (d. 2011)[1]
Gordon Cooper, American aerospace engineer, test pilot and astronaut (Mercury-Atlas 9, Gemini 5), born in Shawnee, Oklahoma (d. 2004)
Patrick Baudry, French astronaut (STS-51-G) and second French citizen in space, born in Douala, Cameroon
Dutch and English army led by Maurice of Nassau captures heavily protected city of Breda using a small assault force hidden in a peat barge
Persian polymath Omar Khayyam completes the Jalali calendar, a solar calendar with a precise 33-year intercalation cycle and the basis for the Persian calendar still in use today
England beat Australia to win one of the best cricket series ever, 3-2; Jack Brown hits the fastest 50 in test cricket in 28 mins
Bruce Taylor hits 105 for NZ v India in 1st Test Cricket innings
Soyuz 39 returns to Earth
The Temptations' single "My Girl" reaches #1 on the Billboard Pop Chart; written and produced by the Miracles membersSmokey Robinsonand Ronald White
Cyrano de Bergerac, French playwright and novelist known for his large nose (Voyage to the Moon) and inspiration for Edmond Rostand’s play, born in Paris, France (d. 1655)
Pehr Kalm, Swedish explorer and naturalist, first scientific description of Niagara Falls, born in Ångermanland, Sweden (d. 1779)
(John) "Wes" Montgomery, American influential and Grammy Award-winner jazz guitarist, born in Indianapolis, Indiana (d. 1968)
Pauline Boty, English artist, co-founder of the Pop art movement, born in London (d. 1966)
Tyler, the Creator [Tyler Okonma], American rapper and record producer (Flower Boy; Igor), born in Ladera Heights, California
Momoko Tsugunaga, Japanese J-pop singer (Berryz Kobo), actress, radio personality, and kindergarten teacher, born in Kashiwa, Chiba, Japan
Barry Sadler's "Ballad of the Green Berets" becomes #1 (13 weeks)
US naval boat "Cyclops" disappears in Bermuda Triangle
Ghana (formerly Gold Coast) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Princess Theater (Latin Quarter, Cotton Club) opens at 200 W 48th NYC
9,000 residents are evacuated from Wagga Wagga, Australia, as the Murrimbidgee River threatens to overflow
Portuguese navigatorFerdinand Magellanis the first European to sight Guam in the western Pacific
Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is patented byFelix Hoffmannat the German company Bayer
Tibetan spiritual leaderDalai Lamais vaccinated against COVID-19 in Dharamsala, India and urges others to do so
Ben Harney, American composer and "Father of Ragtime Piano" (You've Been a Good Old Wagon, But You Done Broke Down), born on the Mississippi River (d. 1938)[1]

Robert "Lefty" Grove, American Baseball HOF pitcher (6 × MLB All-Star; World Series 1929, 30; AL MVP 1931; Triple Crown 1930, 31; Philadelphia A's, Boston Red Sox), born in Lonaconing, Maryland (d. 1975)
Bob Wills, American western-swing singer, and bandleader, known as the 'King of Western Swing' (Texas Playboys -"Steel Guitar Rag"), born in Limestone County, Texas (d. 1975)
Madge Adam, English solar astronomer (1st solar physicist at Oxford university's observatory), born in London, England (d. 2001)
Will Eisner, American illustrator and pioneering cartoonist (The Spirit), born in  Kolomyia, Austria-Hungary (d. 2005)
Mary Wilson, American pop vocalist (Supremes - "Where Did Our Love Go?"), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 2021)[1]
Maurice Ashley, American chess player and commentator (1st Black Grandmaster), born in St. Andrews, Jamaica

Julio Bocca, Argentine ballet dancer (American Ballet Theater, 1986-2006; Ballet Argentino, 1990-2007), and artistic director (National Ballet of Uruguay, 2010-18), born in Munro, Buenos Aires, Argentina
Carla McGhee, American basketball forward (Olympic gold 1996; NCAA C'ship Uni of Tennessee 1987, 89), born in Peoria, Illinois
Pretty Yende, South African operatic soprano, born in Piet Retief, Mpumalanga, South Africa
